violations.text: The doors to the outside, located in stairway C and stairway to the left of cafeteria, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/18/2025
The facility is lacking test strips to measure the concentration of the quat & chlorine sanitizing solution for the 3 bay sink and dishwasher. New Violation. Correct By: 03/31/2026

The doors to the outside, located in stairway C and stairway to the left of cafeteria, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 11/18/2025

The door to the outside, located in the (stairwell C ), is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2025
The non-food contact surfaces of the (food cabinet shelves ) have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2025
The (wall ) in the (cafeteria) area is in poor repair. Observed a hole in wall The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 04/09/2025

Inspector observed uncovered bread(placed on liner) being stored near warewashing machine. Per PIC, bread was placed there for prepping. PIC informed to cease practice and removed bread from area. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 09/19/2024

The floors under ware washing area has an accumulation of soil residue and old rodent droppings. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. Repeat Violation. Correct By: 09/19/2024

The floors under ware washing area has an accumulation of soil residue and old rodent droppings. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 03/11/2024

The establishment does not employ a full-time, on-site manager certified in food safety at all times when time temperature control for safety (TCS) food is being prepared, handled, and/or served. New Violation. Correct By: 11/16/2023

The presence of small flying insects is not controlled as evidenced by an abundance of small flying insects observed in kitchen area. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. New Violation. Correct By: 11/16/2023

The floors and walls throughout the cafeteria and beneath baseboards have an accumulation of soil residue and/or food debris. The physical facilities shall be cleaned as often as necessary to keep them clean. New Violation. Correct By: 05/16/2023
